beats.activity: Wake up, pour-over coffee ritual, check weather and work plan for the day.
beats.reasoning: Kwame starts his day at home with his signature pour-over coffee ritual. Yesterday's energy was moderate (45/100), so a calm, intentional morning fits his laid-back style. The drizzle outside makes staying home longer appealing.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland, navigating around SR-520 closure by taking I-90 westbound detour.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so Kwame leaves early (8am) to avoid the rush and takes the I-90 detour. He works at a custom furniture shop in Kirkland but given the closure impact, this beat reflects the longer commute buffer time needed. Steady rain from 7am–2pm makes early departure sensible.

beats.activity: Work on custom furniture commission in the shop; focused on joinery and finishing details.
beats.reasoning: Kwame settles into deep work on the furniture piece that's been consuming him. The rainy morning is perfect for focused indoor work. He's made good time on the commute, so he can sink into the craft before lunch.

beats.activity: Lunch at a local Kirkland spot; stay dry and warm during the rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at noon, so Kwame grabs lunch at a familiar spot he visited yesterday. Staying indoors and warm is practical. He's unbothered by routine and doesn't mind revisiting places he likes.

beats.activity: Return to shop and continue work; rain eases by 2pm, so focus remains steady.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block while rain clears. Kwame is in flow state with the commission and will push through until late afternoon. The furniture piece is his priority right now.

beats.activity: Drive home from Kirkland; rain has cleared, evening light is good.
beats.reasoning: By 5pm the rain has stopped (cleared by 2pm) and SR-520 is back open. Kwame takes the normal route back across the lake. The drive is smooth and he's ready to be home.

beats.activity: Stop by Goodwill (Capitol Hill) to hunt for interesting wood samples and vintage finds.
beats.reasoning: Thrifting is one of Kwame's favorite pastimes. The rain has cleared, so a quick stop on the way home is appealing. He's already found useful wood samples here before and keeps an eye out for inspiration.

beats.activity: Walk home through Capitol Hill, enjoying the clear evening air.
beats.reasoning: A short, reflective walk home mirrors yesterday's pleasant evening walk. Weather is now clear and the neighborhood feels alive. Kwame values these moments to decompress.

beats.activity: Cook a simple dinner, review joinery notes and sketches for upcoming projects.
beats.reasoning: Kwame settles in at home to cook and spend quiet time with his craft books and notes. This wind-down time is consistent with his laid-back, self-directed energy. He's preparing mentally for tomorrow's work.

beats.activity: Play online chess or review past games with friends; text check-in with close circle.
beats.reasoning: Chess is Kwame's go-to evening activity. He values connection with close friends and will spend time on chess or light socializing from home before winding down for bed.

beats.activity: Sleep; rest after a productive day of focused work and light evening activities.
beats.reasoning: Kwame settles into a full night of sleep after a balanced day. The 8.5-hour sleep window is healthy and reflects his moderate energy recovery. He's earned rest after the focused work block in Kirkland.

diary: Started the day with my pour-over ritual—needed it after hearing about the SR-520 closure. The detour added twenty minutes, but I got to the shop by nine and settled into the joinery work pretty quick. There's something about a grey, rainy morning that makes you focus. Grabbed lunch at Cafe Juanita around noon; the rain was still coming down, so I stayed put and watched the water stream down the windows with my coffee. Afternoon felt lighter once I got back to the bench—the piece is coming together nicely, and by the time I left at three, the clouds had mostly cleared. Swung by Goodwill on the walk home, found some nice reclaimed walnut offcuts that'll be perfect for inlays. Cooked something simple, sketched out notes for the next commission. Played a few chess games before bed. Productive, quiet day.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure could've thrown me off, but honestly it just meant I left earlier and leaned into the focus. There's a rhythm to working through grey weather—fewer distractions, fewer reasons to rush. Lunch was deliberate; I know the rain will pass, so I didn't stress. By afternoon my mood had lifted naturally with the weather clearing. The Goodwill stop was an impulse, but it paid off—those wood samples sparked ideas I've been sitting on. I felt purposeful today, not scattered. The evening wind-down with chess felt earned.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
